data.title: Quantum Computing News: IonQ (IONQ) Spends $1.8 Billion to Lock In Chip Supply and Cut Costs
data.description:
Welcome to Monday's update on quantum computing. As 2026 moves ahead, the market focus is shifting away from raw lab gains and toward tools, deals, and early business use. This update highlights recent moves that matter for investors tracking the quantum space.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Let's begin.

1. Software Takes the Lead at Microsoft

First, Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) outlined a clear shift toward software as the next step in quantum progress. The company said the field is moving from fragile physical qubits toward logical qubits that rely on error correction. Microsoft technical fellow Matthias Troyer wrote that useful quantum systems will need strong software alongside new hardware.

The Microsoft Quantum Development Kit now works as a full tool set that runs on a normal laptop and links with Visual Studio Code and GitHub Copilot. Microsoft said its goal is to meet developers where they already work and lower the skill barrier. Troyer wrote that the tools help researchers design and test programs today while preparing for larger systems later. As a result, quantum work is being treated more like modern software work than a niche science task.

2. IonQ Moves Toward Vertical Control

Next, IonQ Inc. (IONQ) announced plans to buy SkyWater Technology Inc. (SKYT) for about $1.8 billion. Under the deal, SkyWater holders will get $15 in cash and $20 in IonQ stock per share. IonQ Chief Executive Niccolo de Masi said the deal will form a vertically integrated quantum platform business. SkyWater will stay a neutral foundry and serve other clients after the deal closes. IonQ said the move should shorten build times and lower costs as demand grows. The deal also fits with rising U.S. policy support for chips and quantum work. Even so, IonQ shares fell 4.22% on the day, showing that investors remain cautious about near-term returns.

3. Post-Quantum Security Gains Urgency

Meanwhile, Arqit Quantum Inc. (ARQQ) launched Encryption Intelligence as a full commercial product. The company said the tool helps firms find and track weak or outdated encryption across networks and cloud systems. Arqit said many firms still lack a full view of their crypto risks, which raises costs and delays future upgrades. The company is also taking part in the UK NCSC post-quantum pilot to test its approach. Chief Security Officer Trevor Graham said, "Organizations cannot manage what they cannot see."

As quantum risk planning moves closer to real budgets, tools like this are becoming part of normal security planning.

4. Early Use Cases Show Mixed Signals

Finally, new claims around quantum-aided trading drew attention at the Centre for Frontier Technologies and Innovation event on January 21. A group reported a 34% gain in forecast accuracy using quantum tools in trading models. The result points to progress beyond theory, but scale remains a key limit. Experts said real returns still depend on cost, energy use, and stable systems. For now, most gains remain narrow and hard to repeat. Still, these tests help shape where quantum may first add value in finance.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to line up all the stocks appearing in the piece alongside quantum computing stocks. It's a quick way to see how they stack up and where the field could be heading.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Mixed as Investors Await Big Tech Earnings, Gold Tops $5,100
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded mixed on Monday morning, as investors look forward to Big Tech earnings this week, including reports from Microsoft (MSFT), Meta Platforms (META), and Apple (AAPL). Electric vehicle (EV) maker Tesla (TSLA) will also announce its Q4 2025 results this week. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were down 0.23% and 0.06%, respectively, while futures tied to the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up by 0.02% at 8:15 a.m. EST on January 26.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, gold touched a fresh all-time high, crossing $5,100 an ounce on Monday. The yellow metal extended its stellar run as investors favored the safe-haven asset amid geopolitical woes related to Greenland and Venezuela, as well as global volatility. Moreover, the trade tensions between the U.S. and Canada have resurfaced. U.S. President Donald Trump threatened to slap 100% tariffs on Canada if it signs a trade deal with China. However, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said on Sunday that his country has no intention of entering into a free trade agreement with Beijing.

All eyes are on key earnings reports from over 90 S&P 500 companies this week, including Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Apple, and Tesla. Other major earnings include reports from Boeing (BA), UnitedHealth (UNH), AT&T (T), International Business Machines (IBM), Starbucks (SBUX), ASML Holding (ASML), ServiceNow (NOW), Visa (V), Mastercard (MA), SoFi Technologies (SOFI), Exxon Mobil (XOM), and Chevron (CVX).

Investors will also focus on the U.S. Federal Reserve's first policy decision of 2026 on Wednesday. While the central bank is expected to keep its overnight rate intact, traders will look for insights into the Fed officials' plans for potential rate cuts ahead.

Turning to trending stocks, USA Rare Earth (USAR) shares surged 14% in Monday's pre-market trading after the Trump administration took a stake in the rare earths miner. The company will issue 16.1 million shares of common stock and 17.6 million shares in warrants. Also, Allied Gold (AAUC) stock was up about 4%, as the gold miner agreed to be acquired by Hong Kong-based Zijin Gold (HK:2259) for C$5.5 billion in cash.

Additionally, Newmont (NEM) stock was up 4.6% in pre-market trading as gold prices continued to trend higher amid geopolitical uncertainty. In contrast, Enphase Energy (ENPH) shares declined more than 2% after the solar energy company said that it would cut its workforce by more than 5%. Plus, Revolution Medicines (RVMD) stock plunged nearly 20% after the Wall Street Journal reported that Merck (MRK) ended its talks to acquire the cancer-drug biotech.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Dow Jones Climbs as Government Shutdown Odds Rip Higher
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) opened the trading week higher, despite surging odds of a government shutdown by January 31. The odds have jumped to 81% from 10% on prediction platform Polymarket since Friday.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Over the weekend, the fatal shooting of another protester in Minneapolis by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agent led to Senate Democrats threatening to block a $1.2 trillion spending bill unless funding for the U.S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is removed from it.

Will There Be Another Government Shutdown?

The bill needs a total of 60 votes to pass, and Senate Democrats have communicated that they won't support it. The group has asked for the DHS to be reformed amid mass public outcry over the shooting.

"The appalling murders of Renee Good and Alex Pretti on the streets of Minneapolis must lead Republicans to join Democrats in overhauling ICE and CBP to protect the public," said Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer.

However, the severity of the shutdown could be limited if Republicans choose to hold back on a DHS funding vote while voting for the other measures in the bill separately.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: This Is Why Brand Engagement Network Stock (BNAI) Rocketed Today
data.description:
Brand Engagement Network (BNAI) stock was on the rise again Monday after the artificial intelligence (AI) company caught the interest of retail traders. Increased trading activity has been ongoing for BNAI stock after the company announced the finalization of a strategic partnership with Valio Technologies last week.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This agreement will have Brand Engagement Network establish an exclusive AI licensing framework for government and commercial markets across Africa. This includes a memorandum of understanding with Nelson Mandela University for a "regulated, institution-approved pilot deployment of artificial intelligence designed to enhance student wellbeing."

Retail traders appear to have taken notice of this deal in early morning trading on Monday, resulting in heightened trading activity today. As of this writing, more than 3.9 million shares have changed hands. While this is still below the company's three-month daily average of 14.26 million units, investors will note that this movement came before the opening bell today. Additionally, the stock saw some 44 million shares change hands on Friday.

Brand Engagement Network Stock Movement Today

Brand Engagement Network stock was up 241.08% in pre-market trading on Monday, following a 90.3% rally on Friday. The shares have increased 610.34% year-to-date and 244.84% over the past 12 months. Investors will also note that the stock has rallied 171.95% over the past five days.
data.author: William White

data.title: Stock Bulls Rejoice as ServiceNow Selloff 'Looks Overdone,' Says Analyst
data.description:

data.title: 3 Best Stocks to Buy Today, 1/26/2026, According to Top Analysts
data.description:
Looking for top-rated stocks to buy now? Recent analyst updates highlight three companies that have earned Strong Buy ratings from Top Wall Street analysts. Each stock offers solid upside potential, supported by fresh Buy calls.

For more stock ideas, check out TipRanks' Analyst Top Stocks tool, which tracks the latest Buy ratings from top analysts in real time.

Here are today's top analyst-backed stock picks. Click on any ticker to research the company before deciding whether to add it to your portfolio.

Exagen (XGN) – Exagen develops blood-based diagnostic tests for autoimmune diseases such as lupus and rheumatoid arthritis. Today, Canaccord Genuity analyst Kyle Mikson maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a price target of $15 per share. In the last three months, all four Top Analysts covering the stock have rated it a Buy. Taken together, their 12-month price targets imply an upside of about 204.35%.

Vertical Aerospace (EVTL) – Vertical Aerospace designs and develops electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft for urban air mobility and short-haul air travel. Today, William Blair analyst Louie DiPalma maintained a Buy rating on the stock. In the last three months, all three Top Analysts covering the stock have rated it a Buy. Taken together, their 12-month price targets imply an upside of about 96.43%.

Par Technology (PAR) – PAR Technology provides cloud-based software and hardware solutions for restaurants and hospitality businesses. Today, BTIG analyst Andrew Harte maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a price target of $60 per share. In the last three months, all four Top Analysts covering the stock have rated it a Buy. Taken together, their 12-month price targets imply an upside of about 101.74%.

Who Are the Top Analysts? 

TipRanks ranks financial analysts according to the success rates of their ratings and the average return on each of their ratings. The Top Analysts have each earned a five-star ranking, thanks to the accuracy and profitability of their ratings over time.
